text:
Market today:
The mood is cautiously optimistic for tech, driven by positive AI investments from Tencent and Nvidia's innovative revenue-sharing model for its compute power. This directly benefits your concentrated tech/chip holdings.

Your holdings:
SOXL is a major concern, down 28.7% and well below its stop. QQQI (-1.86%) and SPAL (-9.58%) are also flagged for attention; SPAL, being leveraged and new, amplifies any drops, especially on margin.

Watch:
Keep a close eye on SOXL for a definitive exit. Also, watch SMH and XLK for potential entry points if the positive chip/tech sentiment holds and scanner signals confirm.

Bottom line:
Prioritize managing your leveraged, underperforming positions to protect capital, especially given your margin exposure.
plays: Exit SOXL immediately. It's trading significantly below its stop of 200 at 181.47, indicating a clear breach. Cut losses on this leveraged position.
Consider trimming QQQI at 55.36 given its "TRIM" status and proximity to its 52 stop. Protect your income anchor.
With positive chip news, consider a new buy on SMH if it hits its scanner entry of 586.43-584.71, with a stop at 580.88.
